questions.text: The principal value branch of sin^-1(x) is:
questions.explanation: The principal value branch of sin^-1(x) is [-π/2, π/2]. This is the range where sin^-1 is defined.

questions.text: sin^-1(sin x) = x for all real x.
questions.explanation: sin^-1(sin x) = x only when x is in the principal value branch [-π/2, π/2]. For other values, it gives the equivalent angle in this range.
tan^-1(1) = π/4 because tan(π/4) = 1 and π/4 is in the principal value branch (-π/2, π/2).

questions.correctAnswer: Let sin^-1(x) = θ, then x = sin θ. cos(π/2 - θ) = sin θ = x. So cos^-1(x) = π/2 - θ. Therefore, cos^-1(x) + sin^-1(x) = π/2 - θ + θ = π/2
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental identity of inverse trigonometric functions, valid for x ∈ [-1, 1].
